message:
perf(playbook): Optimize blocked content checking in loadMergedPlaybook

Problem:
- loadMergedPlaybook() checked each bullet against all blocked entries
- For each comparison, it computed hashes and ran Jaccard similarity
- O(bullets × blocked) hash computations and tokenizations
- With large playbooks and blocked lists, this became slow

Solution:
- Deduplicate blocked entries by content hash upfront (Map)
- Pre-compute hash and token Set for each unique blocked entry once
- Pre-compute hash and tokens for each bullet being checked
- Use size-ratio fast skip: if maxPossibleIntersection/minPossibleUnion
  is below threshold (0.85), skip the full Jaccard computation
- Only compute actual intersection/union for candidates that pass filter

Removed:
- Unused isBlockedContent() async helper function (inlined logic with
  optimizations directly in loadMergedPlaybook)

This reduces blocked content checking from O(n×m×k) to O(n×m) with
significant constant factor improvement from pre-computation and
fast-skip filtering, where n=bullets, m=blocked entries, k=avg tokens.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
